body_txt: Everyone loves the inspirational story of the Self-Made Man or Woman whose rise to power, wealth and notoriety from humble beginnings is through their own efforts. However, the story is often more inspirational than the truth. The Self-Made Lie is when someone who has cultivated a story around themselves where they fought their way to the top and earned their wealth, power, and position in society, but in reality they had a head start with advantages that were kept secret or otherwise a lesser-known fact. In contrast, some people pretend to have come from a privileged background due to shame over their middle or lower-class roots or hype up a modest fortune that isn't as grand as they claim . At the most innocent, the origins of their wealth or reputation is not sheer pluck and hard work but they simply got a lucky break at the right time, was given a helping hand that they kept secret , other assistance they did not know of at the time (inherited wealth, favors from friends or family) or were engaged in something less savory and happened to be given credit for a noble thing . At the most insidious, their success is due to exploitation of a true mastermind, even cheating them out of their deserved credit and accomplishments and lead to Stolen Credit Backfire once they can’t lie their way out of a situation. This can also result in a Broken Pedestal and is common in stories about a Liar Revealed . Sometimes, the beneficiary of the lie may actually delude themselves into thinking their accomplishments are due solely to their own efforts . This trope is quite common in stories that deconstruct the myth of pulling yourself up from your bootstraps, showing that wealthy people are often born wealthy (or at least upper-middle-class), and their parents were their earliest investors, or at least provided them with connections. Or, if they really were born poor, then they got rich through insidious means. The Self-Made Myth can overlap with the Self-Made Lie, as the former is where they create a mythical reputation about themselves to inspire fear or admiration, while the latter is presenting themselves as one who overcame disadvantaged origins to rise to the top. One tends to feed the other in some way. Compare Mock Millionaire , Miles Gloriosus , Phony Veteran , Fake Ultimate Hero and Hard Work Fallacy , which underlines the story the person is trying to cultivate. See Based on a Great Big Lie for cases where a purportedly real event that brings someone fame and fortune turns out to be fictitious. See Self-Censored Release and I Was Young and Needed the Money for similar tropes about someone trying to hide behind the truths of their past. Due to the controversial nature behind whether or not successful people became successful on their own merits or not, there will be No Real Life Examples, Please!
